summary: Decomp/matching PR centered on Old Pupupu stage code. It implements previously stubbed groldpupupu functions, adds stage data and local data tables, replaces several gv.unk accesses with typed oldpupupu/oldpupupu2 GroundVars, and makes small formatting-only edits in related gr stage files. The only loaded feedback is an automated decomp report showing matched-code/data improvements and two new matches.

key_files.role: Primary implementation file; adds includes, stage data, static tables, two large function bodies, typed GroundVars access, and a narrow noinline wrapper.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/groldpupupu.c added 383 deleted 28 hunks 14
diff_excerpt: groldpupupu.c adds grOp_803E6748 StageData, grOldPupupuSpawnDesc, grOp_803E67B0, grOp_803E67D8, grOp_803E67E4
diff_excerpt: replaces /// #grOldPupupu_80210D10 and /// #grOldPupupu_802113E0 with C implementations
diff_excerpt: replaces gp->gv.unk accesses with gp->gv.oldpupupu and gp->gv.oldpupupu2

key_files.role: Header/type update; adds Old Pupupu ground variable structs and union arms.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/types.h added 18 deleted 0 hunks 2
raw_diff: adds struct grOldPupupu_GroundVars with xC4..xE0 s32 fields
raw_diff: adds struct grOldPupupu_GroundVars2 with s16 xC4 and xC6
raw_diff: adds oldpupupu and oldpupupu2 to Ground gv union

key_files.role: Incidental formatting changes in existing Old Kongo code.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/groldkongo.c added 4 deleted 6 hunks 3
diff_excerpt: line wrapping/indentation changes around DegToRad expression, distance expression, and temp_r3_8 declaration

key_files.role: Incidental formatting change in existing Kinoko Route code.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grkinokoroute.c added 2 deleted 1 hunks 1
diff_excerpt: wraps Ground_801C3DB4 assignment to spawn_idx

what_changed: Added Old Pupupu stage-owned data in groldpupupu.c, including the /GrOp.dat StageData record, animation index tables, spawn descriptor table, phase order table, and two Vec3 direction vectors.

Implemented grOldPupupu_80210D10 with timer decrement/respawn logic, random spawn selection, camera bounds, natural for-loop over spawned objects, JObj translation setup, animation start, and JOBJ_HIDDEN clearing.

Implemented grOldPupupu_802113E0 with a three-case state machine using gp->gv.oldpupupu fields, animation transitions, paired state propagation to Ground_801C2BA4(1), camera/audio/effect calls, and side-dependent behavior.
Recovered typed GroundVars for Old Pupupu in types.h and changed groldpupupu.c from gp->gv.unk/raw cast access to gp->gv.oldpupupu and gp->gv.oldpupupu2 access.
Changed fn_802112F4 to use GET_GROUND(gobj) and gp->gv.oldpupupu.xDC instead of a void* temporary and gp->gv.unk.xDC.
Added a pragma-scoped grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline wrapper and calls it from grOldPupupu_80210D10.
Added includes needed by the recovered code, including camera, grdisplay, lbaudio_ax, baselib/debug, gobjgxlink, and gobjproc.
Made small formatting-only edits in grkinokoroute.c and groldkongo.c.
smart_moves: Introduced explicit GroundVars structs and union arms instead of continuing to use gp->gv.unk and a raw s16 cast, improving reviewability while staying local to the ground/stage type system.

Used project-level helpers/macros such as GET_GROUND, HSD_ASSERT, HSD_JObjSetTranslate*, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, grAnime_801C7FF8, and grAnime_801C8138 rather than leaving expanded or raw generated patterns in the shown code.
Expressed repeated spawns in grOldPupupu_80210D10 as a natural for loop over count, matching the loaded natural-loops standard.
Kept the noinline steering tightly scoped with pragma push/pop around a single wrapper, which is preferable to broad pragma changes if the tactic is required.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a stage function repeatedly accesses gp->gv.unk fields with consistent offsets, adding a stage-specific GroundVars struct and union arm can turn generated-looking storage into typed, reviewable source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds grOldPupupu_GroundVars and grOldPupupu_GroundVars2
diff_excerpt: gp->gv.unk.xC4/xC8/xCC/xD0/xD4/xD8/xDC/xE0 replaced with gp->gv.oldpupupu fields
diff_excerpt: *(s16*) &gp->gv.unk.xC4 replaced with gp->gv.oldpupupu2.xC4

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header-level type recovery may be part of a matching PR when the body proves the access surface; here the code and types.h were updated together for Old Pupupu GroundVars.
evidence_refs: changed_files: types.h changed with groldpupupu.c
raw_diff: union gains oldpupupu and oldpupupu2
diff_excerpt: groldpupupu.c uses the new union arms

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovered stage behavior can combine code and stage-owned data tables; however, data-table changes should be tied to the function work or explicit report improvements rather than treated as generic data cleanup.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds grOp_803E6748, grOp_803E67B0, grOp_803E67D8, grOp_803E67E4
text_corpus: bot report shows groldpupupu .data improved from 48.13% to 86.86%, .sdata from 61.54% to 80.88%, and .sdata2 from 13.33% to 100.00%

diff_excerpt: grOldPupupu_802113E0

naming_conventions.name: oldpupupu and oldpupupu2
naming_conventions.meaning: Ground gv union arms for Old Pupupu state; oldpupupu2 is used for the s16 timer-like xC4 field in the shown code.

naming_conventions.name: grOldPupupuSpawnDesc
naming_conventions.meaning: Local typedef for entries in the Old Pupupu spawn descriptor table with x0, x1, and x2 fields; semantic field names were not recovered in this PR slice.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: typedef struct grOldPupupuSpawnDesc { s8 x0; s8 x1; s16 x2; }
diff_excerpt: grOp_803E67B0[10] initialized and indexed in grOldPupupu_80210D10

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a tightly scoped noinline wrapper when direct helper calls do not produce the desired call shape.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline
diff_excerpt: grOldPupupu_80210D10 calls grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline(2)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The loaded PR evidence does not include an author explanation or objdiff snippet proving why this wrapper was required; only the automated report shows score improvements.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace expanded JObj/assert logic with project macros and inlines when the source-level operation is clear.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_ASSERT(0x216, jobj)
diff_excerpt: HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z and HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll are used in grOldPupupu_80210D10
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review discussion confirms the exact matching rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use natural loops for repeated object spawning where each iteration only changes index-derived animation offset and x-position.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: for (i = 0; i < count; i++) loop in grOldPupupu_80210D10
diff_excerpt: x += step and grAnime_801C7FF8(... 40.0F * (f32) i ...)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The report gives function score improvements but not full function matches for grOldPupupu_80210D10.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed union fields and GET_GROUND to reduce raw pointer/void* artifacts.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_802112F4 changes gp = (new_var = gobj->user_data) to gp = GET_GROUND(gobj)
diff_excerpt: switch changes from gp->gv.unk.xDC to gp->gv.oldpupupu.xDC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Bot report shows fn_802112F4 improved to 100%, but it does not isolate which source change caused the match.

notes: The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not available.

The raw diff loaded inline is truncated; targeted local grep recovered the types.h hunk, but this intake does not claim exhaustive line-by-line coverage of the full patch beyond changed-file metadata and visible hunks.
The automated report supports score improvements and two new matches, but it is not a full build/objdiff ledger and does not prove which tactic caused each score change.
grOldPupupu_80210D10 and grOldPupupu_802113E0 are reported as improved, not fully matched, so tactics from those functions should be treated as candidates rather than accepted matching standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed GroundVars can replace gv.unk/raw casts when a stage-specific access pattern is recovered
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2437 added grOldPupupu_GroundVars and grOldPupupu_GroundVars2 to src/melee/gr/types.h and changed groldpupupu.c accesses from gp->gv.unk and a raw s16 cast to gp->gv.oldpupupu/oldpupupu2 fields. This is a concrete example of typed-fields-over-pointer-math in the ground stage subsystem.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grOldPupupu_GroundVars structs and union arms
diff_excerpt: gp->gv.unk replacements in grOldPupupu_802109D8, grOldPupupu_80210A24, grOldPupupu_8021119C, fn_802112F4

accepted_candidate_records.title: If noinline is required, isolate it around a wrapper
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2437 used pragma push/pop and dont_inline around only grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline, then called that wrapper from grOldPupupu_80210D10. The loaded evidence supports this as a narrow tactic pattern, but not as proof that the tactic is broadly necessary.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline pragma block
diff_excerpt: call from grOldPupupu_80210D10

source_update_candidates.title: Old Pupupu stage data path and StageData symbol in groldpupupu.c
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2437 shows src/melee/gr/groldpupupu.c defining char grOp_803E673C[] = "/GrOp.dat" and StageData grOp_803E6748 with stage ID OLDPUPUPU and grOp_803E6688 callbacks.

diff_excerpt: StageData grOp_803E6748 = { OLDPUPUPU, grOp_803E6688, grOp_803E673C, ... }
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path/source fact for the stage data registry; route to curator because PR intake must not promote path facts directly.

source_update_candidates.title: Narrow pragma/noinline wrapper examples should require explicit verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR contains a tightly scoped dont_inline wrapper around grOldPupupu_802108B4_noinline and an automated report showing progress, but no author note explaining the need. Curator may consider adding this as an example under existing pragma/register/asm or matching-tactics standards: acceptable only with narrow scope and verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around one wrapper
text_corpus: bot report shows grOldPupupu_80210D10 improved to 85.46%, not fully matched
source_update_candidates.reason: The example is useful but evidence is insufficient to promote as a new accepted standard without curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that grOldPupupu_80210D10 or grOldPupupu_802113E0 fully matched; the bot report lists them as improvements to 85.46% and 87.16%, respectively.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are present.
Do not promote the added data tables as globally correct data ownership facts solely from this intake; the report shows data improvements, but no section ownership discussion is loaded.
Do not treat the noinline wrapper as normal style; it is a narrow tactic with incomplete verifier evidence in this slice.
